cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user is asking about what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. First, I need to go through the passage again to make sure I understand the context correctly. The author was with his mother when they met Mr. Lee. The author didn't pay attention to Mr. Lee because he thought he could see him anytime in the neighborhood. After passing Mr. Lee, the mother scolded the author, saying that even a dog wags its tail when passing by. So the mother is teaching the author to greet everyone, not to ignore people. The question is asking what the author did when he met Mr. Lee. The options are A to D. Let's look at each option. Option D says he ignored him. The passage says he "didn't pay any attention to him," which is the same as ignoring. The other options don't fit. A says he talked to someone else, but the story doesn't mention that. B mentions a dog, but the author didn't get distracted by a dog. C
